subject: Re: peristent error
body:
the new version seems to do much better, with fewer crashes. Thank you. 

On Tuesday, October 14, 2025 at 11:38:50 AM UTC+1 Wanlong Hu wrote:

> You are welcome. You might find this discussion 
> <https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/2Vsz7rruGlE> helpful as well. I 
> just found my reconstruction overfitting a lot...
>
> 在2025年10月14日星期二 UTC+8 17:06:23<peter.p.c...@gmail.com> 写道：
>
>> thank you, we will try updating! BTW, it is definitely not due to memory. 
>> I get this error now even with a single species, which is just over 400 
>> subtomos. The same set refines just fine in Relion. 
>>
>> On Tuesday, October 14, 2025 at 9:14:13 AM UTC+1 Wanlong Hu wrote:
>>
>>> You might encounter the same issue discussed here 
>>> <https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/4shNQFKYnv8/m/hq4bFu8fAQAJ>. I 
>>> solved this by updating to dev36.
>>>
>>>
>>> 在2025年10月14日星期二 UTC+8 07:05:56<peter.p.c...@gmail.com> 写道：
>>>
>>>> I am getting a persistent error: 
>>>>
>>>> Unhandled exception. System.NotImplementedException: The method or 
>>>> operation is not implemented.
>>>>    at MCore.MCore.WorkerDied(Object sender, EventArgs e) in 
>>>> /home/runner/micromamba/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1750964440471/work/MCore/MCore.cs:line 
>>>> 599
>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.ReportDeath() in 
>>>> /home/runner/micromamba/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1750964440471/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
>>>> 264
>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.<StartHeartbeat>b__14_0() in 
>>>> /home/runner/micromamba/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1750964440471/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
>>>> 190
>>>> /tmp/slurmd/job32468010/slurm_script: line 26: 2220026 Aborted         
>>>>         (core dumped) MCore --population m/Cores.population 
>>>> --refine_imagewarp 1x1 --refine_particles
>>>>
>>>> Am I running out of memory? 
>>>> I have ~4000 subtomos (two species), refinement is at ~10A, particle 
>>>> size is 250A for one and 330A for the other species. Data are binned to 
>>>> 3.02 A/pix. I am using one GPU on a  node, and no --perdevice_refine switch 
>>>> (in attempt to conserve memory). 
>>>>
>>>> It even fails on  
>>>> MCore --population m/Cores.population --refine_imagewarp 1x1 
>>>> --refine_particles 
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
